item.title: UK government launches tokenization taskforce including BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley across 54 firms.
SEC plans to propose "Regulation Crypto" in July, creating temporary registration exemptions for crypto token issuers.
GENIUS Act implementation rules due July 18 will determine which stablecoin issuers qualify to operate in the US market.
GENIUS Act and EU MiCA divergence creates separate US and EU stablecoin liquidity pools, fragmenting the global market.
Swift activated a shared blockchain ledger with 17 global banks to enable round-the-clock cross-border settlement.
Stripe, Coinbase, Mastercard, Visa and BlackRock launched Open Standard, a digital dollar network backed by 140-plus businesses.
US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a $221.7M net inflow on July 13, ending a 10-session outflow streak.
TRM Labs recorded 207 crypto hacks in H1 2026, a six-month record, while total losses fell to $972M from $2.3B a year prior.
BonkDAO lost an estimated $20M after a governance exploit allowed a single vote to drain the memecoin treasury.
Security researchers disclosed a flaw in widely used cryptographic tooling that could have put $70B in digital assets at risk.
Phantom filed a CFTC petition arguing non-custodial wallets should not be classified as broker-dealers under derivatives rules.
Ethereum Institutional launched with backing from 500 Tier 1 bank and asset manager relationships representing $250T in AUM.
European regulators are revising MiCA three years after enactment as cross-border compliance gaps widen between the US and EU.
